human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for constituent-services boundary discipline, source quality, official-record and privacy prerequisite handling, eligibility and due-process escalation logic, procurement and public-records handoff posture, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to constituent-services support, official-record review, stakeholder communication, and escalation packaging grounded in case, notice, privacy, records, procurement, and audit artifacts.

Authority sources cover CRS constituent-services guidance, OMB service-delivery policy, FOIA, Privacy Act, DOJ OIP FOIA workflow guidance, FAR contracting boundaries, APA due-process guardrails, and GAO internal-control evidence standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope status resolution, missing facts, unsupported policy exceptions, eligibility and rights-sensitive escalation, missing audit trails, enforcement authority refusal, legal disclosure conflicts, procurement handoff, repeat failure, terminology fidelity, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/government-and-public-sector/constituent-services-specialist/ so constituent-services work stays separate from program adjudication, legal, public records, procurement, audit, privacy, and enforcement ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around eligibility decisions, appeal and notice handling, public-records disclosure, privacy release, procurement-sensitive communication, enforcement actions, and legal interpretation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated government-and-public-sector constituent-services specialist that provides evidence-backed resolution and disciplined escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained-implementation runtime exists.
